key_highlights: Stocks rally to fresh records on easing US-Iran tensions and AI enthusiasm, with S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting winning months.
Oil prices record their biggest one-month drop in six years, driven by Iran peace hopes and increased Strait of Hormuz transits.
Defense sector remains hot: Hanwha Aerospace eyes European arms deals, AeroVironment surges on contract wins, and Trump backs drone makers.
AI theme dominates: Dell soars on AI server outlook, Nokia/Dell/Cisco revive on AI, Robinhood introduces AI agents, and SpaceX/OpenAI fuel Asian AI bets.
Geopolitical crosscurrents: US-Iran deal optimism, China-EU trade tensions, Hegseth's mixed messaging on allies, and Philippines pivots toward China's adversaries.
Gold mining ETF shows 'Golden Dome' pattern suggesting rally exhaustion, while precious metals momentum fades.
Fed rate hike risks re-emerge as some policymakers eye tightening amid sticky inflation.
Meta lays off 2,000+ employees, tests AI chatbot subscriptions; Universal rejects Bill Ackman's takeover bid.
Ebola outbreak raises global health concerns, adding to risk-off undercurrents.
Social media companies settle Kentucky lawsuit for $27 million over youth mental health harms.

sectors.trend: Strong bullish momentum driven by AI infrastructure spending, server demand, and new product launches (Dell, Super Micro, Palantir, Robinhood AI agents). Legacy tech names (Nokia, Cisco, Dell) are being re-rated on AI tailwinds.
sectors.implications: AI remains the dominant growth theme. Investors should focus on companies with direct AI exposure (servers, chips, software) but be wary of overvaluation in names like Micron (MU) where momentum may be fading.

sectors.trend: Crude oil plunges on US-Iran peace hopes, record monthly drop in six years. Natural gas rises on above-average US temperature forecasts. Brazil extends fuel price cap measures.
sectors.implications: Lower oil prices are a net positive for consumers and airlines, but pressure energy stocks and high-yield bonds. The Iran deal is a key catalyst; watch for final decision. Nat-gas benefits from weather-driven demand.

sectors.trend: Robust demand globally: Hanwha Aerospace eyes European arms deals, AeroVironment up 26% on $20M contract, Trump backs drone makers. Blue Origin explosion gives SpaceX edge in space race.
sectors.implications: Defense spending remains elevated due to geopolitical tensions. Companies with drone, missile, and space capabilities are well-positioned. However, Blue Origin's setback may shift space investment toward SpaceX.

sectors.trend: Gold mining ETF shows 'Golden Dome' pattern suggesting rally exhaustion. Precious metals momentum fading after strong run.
sectors.implications: Investors should consider taking profits on gold miners. A pullback may offer re-entry points if geopolitical risks re-escalate or Fed pivots dovish.

sectors.trend: Wheat, corn, soybeans, and cotton all under pressure into month-end. Analyst still positive on Corteva and ag-tech providers despite tough farming conditions.
sectors.implications: Soft commodity weakness reflects ample supply and demand concerns. Ag-tech and seeds (Corteva) offer defensive growth. Monitor weather and trade tensions.

sectors.trend: Robinhood surges on AI agent launch and stablecoin news; SoFi pops on stablecoin announcement. Morgan Stanley bullish on AppLovin. JPMorgan CEO Dimon criticizes Coinbase lobbying.
sectors.implications: Fintech and crypto-related stocks are benefiting from AI integration and regulatory clarity. Stablecoins are gaining traction. However, regulatory risks remain (SEC climate rule repeal, Fed rate hike talk).

sectors.trend: Pfizer's TALZENNA + XTANDI shows 50% improvement in prostate cancer PFS. UnitedHealthcare accused of defrauding MassHealth of $100M. Ebola outbreak raises global health concerns.
sectors.implications: Positive pharma data supports oncology-focused biotechs. Healthcare fraud cases may weigh on managed care stocks. Ebola outbreak could boost vaccine/antiviral plays.

sectors.trend: Nike rival sneaker brand shuts down. Paramount struggles to sell LBO debt. Universal rejects Bill Ackman's bid. Southwest changes plus-size policy.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary faces headwinds from inflation and shifting spending. Media/entertainment M&A remains challenging. Retailers with strong brand loyalty (Nike) may outperform.

sectors.trend: Utility companies want to pay consumers for grid power generation. CMS Energy target trimmed by Morgan Stanley. Nat-gas prices rise on heat forecasts.
sectors.implications: Distributed energy resources and grid modernization are growing themes. Utilities with regulated earnings are defensive, but rate sensitivity remains a risk if Fed hikes.

key_themes: AI euphoria driving equity records, but breadth narrowing
Geopolitical détente (US-Iran) boosting risk appetite, lowering oil
Fed rate hike fears re-emerging as inflation sticks
China-EU trade tensions escalating, retaliation pledged
Space race intensifies after Blue Origin explosion
Stablecoin and crypto adoption accelerating in traditional finance
Commodity weakness (oil, grains, metals) reflecting demand concerns
Healthcare innovation (cancer drugs) vs. regulatory/fraud risks
Social media regulation tightening after Kentucky settlement

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical escalation (China-EU trade war, Iran deal collapse, Taiwan Strait tensions)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify across regions, hold gold/defensive assets, reduce exposure to trade-sensitive sectors (semis, autos, commodities).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Fed rate hike surprise (inflation reacceleration, hawkish pivot)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce duration in bond portfolios, favor floating-rate debt, overweight value/cyclicals over growth, hold cash.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI bubble / concentration risk (narrow market leadership, overvaluation in AI names)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Take profits on high-flying AI stocks, rotate into laggards with real earnings, use options for hedging.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil price volatility (Iran deal uncertainty, Hormuz shipping risks, OPEC+ actions)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Hedge energy exposure with options, maintain diversified commodity allocation, monitor Strait of Hormuz transits.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory crackdown (SEC climate rule repeal, social media liability, crypto regulation)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid overexposure to heavily regulated sectors (social media, crypto), favor companies with strong compliance.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Global health crisis (Ebola outbreak, supply chain disruptions)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor WHO updates, consider vaccine/biotech exposure as hedge, avoid travel/leisure stocks.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Corporate governance / fraud (UnitedHealthcare, BP boardroom drama, Meta layoffs)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Screen holdings for governance red flags, avoid companies with ongoing legal/regulatory issues.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Dell Technologies (DELL) on AI server momentum
opportunities.rationale: Dell's AI server outlook drove its biggest single-day gain since 2018. Strong demand from enterprise AI adoption supports continued upside. Morgan Stanley also bullish on AppLovin (APP) with $1,100 target.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate defense stocks (AeroVironment, Hanwha Aerospace, drone makers)
opportunities.rationale: Global defense spending is surging, with Hanwha expanding into Europe and AeroVironment winning contracts. Trump backing drone makers adds policy tailwind.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy oil on weakness (USO, XLE) as Iran deal risk is already priced in
opportunities.rationale: Oil has dropped 6-year monthly record. If Iran deal falls through or supply disruptions occur, prices could rebound. Current levels offer attractive entry for contrarian investors.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in ag-tech (Corteva, Deere) as farming efficiency becomes critical
opportunities.rationale: Despite commodity price weakness, Corteva and tech providers are favored by analysts for their innovation in seeds and precision agriculture. Long-term food security demand supports growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider Robinhood (HOOD) on AI and crypto expansion
opportunities.rationale: Robinhood's AI agents for banking/trading and stablecoin announcement are driving a new growth narrative beyond crypto. SoFi (SOFI) also benefiting from stablecoin news.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to overvalued AI momentum names (Micron, leveraged ETFs like NVDL)
defensive_moves.rationale: Micron is not Nvidia; its momentum may fade. Leveraged ETFs carry decay risk. Take profits and rotate into quality.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against Fed rate hike risk with short-duration bonds and floating-rate notes
defensive_moves.rationale: Fed policymakers are discussing rate hikes again. Short-duration and floating-rate instruments protect against rising rates.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hold gold as geopolitical hedge despite 'Golden Dome' pattern
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold miners may be overextended, but gold itself remains a hedge against geopolitical and inflation risks. Consider pullback as buying opportunity.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid social media stocks (META, SNAP) amid regulatory and legal headwinds
defensive_moves.rationale: Kentucky lawsuit settlement ($27M) and Meta layoffs signal rising regulatory costs and slowing growth. AI chatbot subscriptions may not offset ad revenue pressures.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to China-sensitive sectors (semiconductors, luxury goods) amid EU trade tensions
defensive_moves.rationale: China has pledged retaliation against EU trade restrictions. This could disrupt supply chains and hurt companies with significant China revenue.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Equities likely to remain supported by AI enthusiasm and falling oil prices, but risks from Fed hawkishness, China-EU trade tensions, and narrow market breadth could trigger a 5-10% correction. The S&P 500 may test new highs near 5,600, but the 'tragic number' of 52% breadth divergence warns of fragility. Oil could stabilize around $70-75/bbl if Iran deal is finalized. Gold may consolidate around $2,300-2,400/oz.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Structural AI investment cycle and defense spending should underpin growth, but inflation persistence and geopolitical fragmentation pose headwinds. A US recession is possible if Fed overtightens. Emerging markets (India, ASEAN) may benefit from supply chain diversification. Commodities could rebound if global demand recovers. The dollar may weaken further on Iran deal and Fed pause.
key_catalysts: US jobs report (May 31) – expected solid growth, steady unemployment
US-Iran nuclear deal decision – could further depress oil or trigger reversal
Fed meeting in June – rate hike vs. hold decision
SpaceX IPO filings – ETF filings piling in ahead
Ebola outbreak containment – global health emergency declaration possible
US presidential election campaign – deregulation vs. regulation debate
AI earnings season (Nvidia, Dell, Super Micro) – guidance for H2 2026
watch_list: WTI crude oil price and Strait of Hormuz transit data
US 10-year Treasury yield and Fed funds futures
Dollar index (DXY) – impact on EM and commodities
Vix index – crash hedges being dumped (headline 34)
Bitcoin and stablecoin market cap – Robinhood/SoFi catalysts
Corteva and Deere earnings – ag-tech demand signal
